meta.description: 5-day Cambodia tour: Angkor Wat, Tonle Sap Lake cruise, Phnom Penh Royal Palace, Silver Pagoda, Toul Sleng Museum and Mekong River cruise. From RM2,038 per person. Dhesu Travel, trusted since 1988.
meta.keywords: 5 days Cambodia tour package Malaysia, Siem Reap Phnom Penh 5 day itinerary, Angkor Wat Tonle Sap Phnom Penh tour, complete Cambodia tour Malaysia, Dhesu Cambodia

hero.title: 5 Days: Explore Cambodia.
hero.intro: The most complete Cambodia experience from Malaysia: five days covering everything the country is famous for. Angkor Wat and the full temple circuit in Siem Reap. Tonle Sap Lake — Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake — by boat. The Royal Palace, Silver Pagoda, and Toul Sleng Genocide Museum in Phnom Penh. And the Mekong River at sunset, when the city lights come on and Cambodia looks its most beautiful.
hero.heroImageAlt: Royal Palace Phnom Penh Cambodia riverside capital

includes: Return airport transfers (Siem Reap arrival, Phnom Penh departure)
Meals as per itinerary (B = Breakfast, L = Lunch, D = Dinner)
All tours and sightseeing as per itinerary
Tourist bus transfer Siem Reap to Phnom Penh
excludes: Travel insurance (ETIQA Area 2 Gold from RM41)

overview.heading: 5 Days Cambodia: The Full Picture
body: This is the complete Cambodia experience — the package that leaves nothing important out. Five days covering all the major sites in both of Cambodia's great cities, with time to appreciate each experience properly rather than rushing between them.

Siem Reap takes the first three days. Day 1 is a city orientation and a traditional Apsara dance performance on the first evening. Day 2 is the full Angkor Archaeological Park circuit — Angkor Wat, Angkor Thom, the Bayon, Ta Prohm, and Pre Rup — all day. Day 3 is Tonle Sap Lake: a morning boat cruise through the floating villages of one of the world's most extraordinary inland water environments, followed by the afternoon journey south toward Phnom Penh.

Phnom Penh takes the last two days. Day 4 is a complete immersion in the capital: the Royal Palace compound with the Silver Pagoda and its 5,000 silver floor tiles, the Toul Sleng Genocide Museum in the former S-21 prison complex, the Russian Market, the Independence Monument, and a Mekong River sunset cruise from the famous riverfront esplanade. Day 5 is a free morning before the airport transfer, with enough time for a final walk along the riverfront or a visit to the Central Market.

highlights.heading: Why This 5-Day Cambodia Package Stands Apart

items.title: Angkor Wat & The Full Temple Circuit
items.body: The world's largest religious monument in a full unrushed day. Angkor Wat at sunrise, the walled royal city of Angkor Thom with its 200-faced Bayon, the jungle-reclaimed Ta Prohm (Tomb Raider filming location), and Pre Rup for the most celebrated sunset in Asia. Your guide covers not just the history but the mythology and the story of the civilisation that built all of this.
items.imageAlt: Angkor Wat temple sunrise reflection Cambodia

items.title: Tonle Sap Lake — Life on the Water
items.body: A morning boat cruise through the floating village communities of Chong Kneas on Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake. Floating schools, floating churches, floating petrol stations. Families who have lived on this water for generations. The lake expands from 2,500 to 16,000 square kilometres every wet season as the Mekong reverses its flow — one of the most dramatic seasonal transformations in the natural world.
items.imageAlt: Tonle Sap Lake floating village boat cruise Cambodia

items.title: Royal Palace & Silver Pagoda — Cambodia's Royal Compound
items.body: The Royal Palace compound on Phnom Penh's riverfront contains some of Cambodia's most important buildings. The Throne Hall, built in 1917, is still used for royal coronations and state ceremonies. The Silver Pagoda — named for its floor of 5,000 solid silver tiles weighing one tonne — houses a 17th-century emerald Buddha and a life-size solid gold Buddha studded with 9,584 diamonds. The surrounding gallery walls are painted with a 600-panel mural of the Reamker, Cambodia's version of the Ramayana.
items.imageAlt: Royal Palace Phnom Penh Silver Pagoda Cambodia

items.title: Toul Sleng Genocide Museum — Cambodia's Essential History
items.body: The former Tuol Svay Prey High School was converted by the Khmer Rouge into Security Prison S-21 between 1975 and 1979. Of the estimated 17,000 people held here, fewer than a dozen survived. The museum preserves the site exactly as it was found by Vietnamese forces in 1979: photographs of prisoners, preserved cells, and a meticulous archive. It is a difficult visit and an essential one — the experience that gives the rest of Cambodia its context.
items.imageAlt: Toul Sleng Genocide Museum S-21 Phnom Penh Cambodia

days.title: Arrival Siem Reap — City History Tour | Traditional Dance Dinner
days.description: Arrive at Siem Reap International Airport and transfer to hotel. Afternoon city tour: Wat Preah Prom Rath (the most beautiful temple in Siem Reap city), the twin Preah Ang Chek and Preah Ang Chorm Shrines (the Sisters Temple), Royal Gardens, Wat Thmei Killing Field Memorial, War Memorial Monument. Evening: traditional live performance of classical Cambodian Apsara dances with dinner — the sacred dance depicted in hundreds of bas-reliefs across the Angkor temples, performed by trained dancers in full classical costume to traditional instruments. Overnight in Siem Reap.
suggestedActivities: Siem Reap airport arrival & hotel transfer
Wat Preah Prom Rath city temple
Preah Ang Chek & Preah Ang Chorm Sisters Temple

days.title: Full Day Angkor Archaeological Circuit | Pub Street
days.description: Full day at the Angkor Archaeological Park — the greatest single day of sightseeing in Southeast Asia. Morning: Angkor Wat at sunrise. The 350-metre causeway approach, the outer gallery bas-reliefs (800 metres of carved Hindu mythology), and the five central towers. Angkor Thom South Gate causeway of 108 stone figures; the Bayon with 200 carved faces on 54 towers; Baphuon pyramid temple; Terrace of Elephants; Terrace of the Leper King. Afternoon: Ta Prohm (Tomb Raider temple), Banteay Kdei monastery, Srah Srang royal bathing reservoir, Pre Rup sunset pyramid. Dinner and overnight in Siem Reap. Evening at Pub Street and Night Market.
suggestedActivities: Terrace of Elephants & Terrace of Leper King

days.title: Tonle Sap Lake Cruise | Artisan D'Angkor | Travel to Phnom Penh
days.description: Morning boat cruise on Tonle Sap Lake from Chong Kneas — Southeast Asia's largest freshwater lake and one of the world's most productive inland fisheries. Cruise through the floating village communities: floating houses, floating schools, floating markets. Watch fishermen casting nets and migratory waterbirds feeding in the shallows. Return to Siem Reap for lunch. Visit Artisan D'Angkor Centre — watch craftspeople at work on traditional Khmer silk weaving, lacquerware, stone carving and silver jewellery. After lunch, depart by tourist express bus for Phnom Penh (approximately 6 hours). En route stop at Skuon Market (famous for fried tarantulas and local snacks). Arrive Phnom Penh and transfer to hotel. Dinner on own account.
suggestedActivities: Tonle Sap Lake boat cruise from Chong Kneas
Tourist express bus to Phnom Penh

days.title: Full Day Phnom Penh | Royal Palace | Toul Sleng | Mekong Sunset Cruise
days.description: Full day exploring Cambodia's capital. Morning: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda complex — the Throne Hall, the Silver Pagoda with its 5,000 silver floor tiles, the emerald Buddha, the solid gold Buddha with 9,584 diamonds, and the 600-panel Reamker mural on the surrounding gallery walls. Roung Damrei Museum. Ounalom Pagoda — Cambodia's oldest Buddhist foundation (est. 1443). Independence Monument, King Norodom Sihanouk Statue. Naga World entertainment resort. Afternoon: Wat Phnom hilltop temple (where the city was founded in 1372). Toul Sleng Genocide Museum (former S-21 Khmer Rouge prison complex). Russian Market for silk, silverwork and handicrafts. Evening: sunset cruise on the Mekong River — departing from the riverfront esplanade, passing Diamond Island, fishing villages, and stilted communities as Phnom Penh's lights come on. Dinner and overnight in Phnom Penh.

days.title: Morning at Leisure — Depart Phnom Penh
days.description: Morning at leisure for personal activities or last-minute shopping. The Phnom Penh riverfront esplanade is pleasant in the morning, and the Central Market (Psar Thmei) — one of Asia's finest French art deco market buildings, completed in 1937 — is open early. Transfer to Phnom Penh International Airport for onward journey.

whyBook.heading: The Complete Cambodia Experience — By the Agency That Has Done It Since 1988
whyBook.body: Five days covering Siem Reap and Phnom Penh is the ideal first Cambodia trip. This package leaves nothing important out and gives you time at each site rather than rushing. Dhesu has been arranging this journey for Malaysian travellers since 1988. The logistics, the guides, the hotels, and the local knowledge are all in place.

points.title: MATTA & IATA Registered
points.body: Fully licensed Malaysian travel agency. We handle Cambodia visa documentation guidance, Angkor pass arrangements, and all ground logistics in both cities.

points.title: 35+ Years of Cambodia Experience
points.body: Since 1988, we have been sending Malaysian travellers to Cambodia. Our guides know both cities well — not just the famous sites but the context that makes them meaningful.

points.title: Daily Departures, No Waiting
points.body: Your trip runs on your chosen dates with your own private guide and vehicle. No group minimums. No sharing with strangers.

points.title: Muslim-Friendly Options
points.body: Halal meals arranged at no extra cost in both cities. Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap and Masjid Al-Serkal in Phnom Penh included on request.

sections.heading: 5 Day Cambodia Tour Package from Malaysia
sections.body:
A 5-day Cambodia tour from Malaysia is the most thorough way to experience the country on a first visit. Five days is enough to see the full Angkor circuit, cruise Tonle Sap Lake, visit the Royal Palace and Toul Sleng in Phnom Penh, and have an evening on the Mekong River — without feeling rushed at any point.

This is our most comprehensive Cambodia package and the one that guests most often say left them with the strongest, most complete impression of the country. The combination of ancient temples, living water communities, royal heritage, and the weight of recent history gives you Cambodia in full — not a highlights reel.

sections.heading: Toul Sleng Genocide Museum: Should I Visit?
sections.body:
Toul Sleng is not an easy visit. The former S-21 prison complex where the Khmer Rouge systematically detained and killed an estimated 17,000 people between 1975 and 1979 is preserved almost exactly as it was found. The photographs on the walls, the preserved cells, and the documentation rooms are confronting.

Most visitors say it is essential. Cambodia cannot be fully understood without understanding what happened here and why. The museum is also remarkably well-curated, with survivor testimonies, historical documentation, and context that helps you understand the broader story of the Khmer Rouge period. Plan at least 90 minutes. Your guide will help you navigate both the history and the experience.

sections.heading: Royal Palace and Silver Pagoda: What to Expect
sections.body:
The Royal Palace compound on the Phnom Penh riverfront is one of Southeast Asia's most beautiful royal complexes. The buildings are a combination of French colonial and traditional Khmer architecture, with golden spires visible from across the river. The Silver Pagoda (officially the Temple of the Emerald Buddha) is named for its floor of 5,000 silver tiles, each weighing approximately 1 kilogram. Inside, a 17th-century emerald Buddha sits above a life-size gold Buddha studded with 9,584 diamonds and 2,086 other precious stones.

Dress code is strictly enforced: covered shoulders and knees are required throughout the compound. Sarongs are available for hire at the entrance if needed. Photography is permitted in most areas except inside the Silver Pagoda itself.

items.question: Do Malaysians need a visa for Cambodia?
items.answer: Yes. Malaysian passport holders require a visa to enter Cambodia. Options include the Cambodia e-Visa (apply online before departure, approximately USD 36, processed in 3 business days) or visa on arrival at Siem Reap airport (approximately USD 30, cash only). Dhesu provides full pre-departure guidance on visa options for every Cambodia booking.

items.question: Is 5 days enough for Cambodia?
items.answer: Five days is the ideal first Cambodia trip. It gives you enough time to see the full Angkor circuit, cruise Tonle Sap Lake, and cover Phnom Penh's main sites without rushing any of it. Most guests who do the 5-day itinerary say it felt just right — not compressed, not padded. If you want to add Phnom Kulen National Park (the sacred mountain), we recommend extending to 6 days.

items.question: How do we travel from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh?
items.answer: The Day 3 transfer from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh is by tourist express bus, taking approximately 6 hours on the main highway south. The bus is comfortable (air-conditioned, reclining seats) and includes a stop at Skuon Market en route. For travellers who prefer to fly, a domestic flight takes approximately 40 minutes. Please contact us if you would like to substitute the bus with a flight — there is an additional cost.

items.question: Is the Royal Palace open to visitors?
items.answer: The Royal Palace compound (including the Silver Pagoda) is open to the public daily except during official ceremonies. Entrance costs USD 10 per person, paid on site — this fee is included in the package. Photography is permitted in most areas of the compound. Dress code is strictly enforced: covered shoulders and knees required.

items.question: Is this a private or group tour?
items.answer: The land tour arrangements (guide, vehicles, city tours, boat cruises) are 100% private. You have your own licensed English-speaking guide and private vehicle in both cities. The tourist express bus from Siem Reap to Phnom Penh is a shared coach service used by all tourists on that route.

items.question: Can halal food be arranged throughout the 5 days?
items.answer: Yes. Halal meals can be arranged in both Siem Reap and Phnom Penh at no extra cost. Mosque visits — Masjid AN NEAKMAH in Siem Reap and Masjid Al-Serkal in Phnom Penh — can be included in your itinerary on request.
